It's a single command line, albeit with semicolons? We must not chain with && or ;; but we can use if statement with ;. But we can't do conditional in shell across commands unless we use script.

But the policy says "Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time. Do NOT chain with && ; or multiline scripts". That means we cannot include ; inside because that's a chain. But using if statement includes ; which would be chain. Might be disallowed.

We could run the check first, then if exit code indicates not installed, run install command. But we can decide to always run the install command; dnf will skip if already installed. That is simpler and idempotent. So we can run "dnf install -y subscription-manager". That will install if not present; if already present, no changes.

Thus plan: Run "dnf install -y subscription-manager". No config file modifications.

We need to read_file? There's no config to modify. The rule is installing the package. So no config changes. We can skip read_file. But requirement: ALWAYS use read_file to inspect the target config file BEFORE modifying it. But we are not modifying any config file. Might still okay.

So plan description: Install package via dnf -y subscription-manager.

We need to ensure not to chain multiple commands. The if statement runs in shell, still one command.

Let's proceed.
